basket_summary.narrative: The watchlist is mixed but not dead. 26 of 44 symbols are still above the 200 SMA, 28 are above the 50 SMA, and 28 have RSI above 50. 6 have just reclaimed the 20 EMA, while 5 are clean enough for the first-pass screen. In plain English: there are a few names that look worth watching, but this is not a crowd of obvious buys.

symbols.story: ABG.JO is holding up well. The bigger trend and the shorter-term trend are both pointing the right way, so this is a name that can stay on the buy-watch list. Volume confirmation is adequate, which helps the case. MACD is confirming improving momentum.
symbols.caution: If momentum cools off, this can turn from solid to ordinary pretty quickly.
symbols.strengthen_next: Hold above the 20 EMA and keep pushing away from the 50 SMA.
symbols.weaken_next: Slip back under the 20 EMA and then lose the 50 SMA.

symbols.notes: Current open holdings have an average purchase price of 3312. Trimmed position by 50%
symbols.story: ADH.JO is one of the cleaner names today. It is holding above both the long- and medium-term trend lines, RSI is on the healthy side of 50, and price has just reclaimed the 20 EMA. That makes it more of a buy-watch than a back-away name right now. MACD is still leaning bearish, so momentum repair is incomplete.
symbols.caution: This is still early. If it slips back under the 20 EMA, the short-term case weakens fast.
symbols.strengthen_next: Stay above the 20 EMA, keep RSI above 50, and build more space above the 50 SMA.
symbols.weaken_next: Lose the 20 EMA quickly or let RSI fall back below 50.

symbols.story: ART.JO is sending mixed signals, so there is no clean edge yet. It is not ugly enough to ignore completely, but it is not strong enough to call a buy setup either. Momentum is neutral and volume confirmation is light. MACD is still leaning bearish, so momentum repair is incomplete.

symbols.weaken_next: Let the weaker trend layer take control of the chart.
